2605.27164.title: Query Symbolically or Retrieve Semantically? A Dataset and Method for Semi-Structured Question Answering
2605.27164.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for question answering typically retrieve evidence by semantic similarity between the query and document chunks. While effective for unstructured text, this approach is less reliable on semi-structured corpora where answering may require exact filtering, aggregation, or exhaustive retrieval over structured attributes across multiple documents. Symbolic approaches support such operations, but they are often brittle on noisy natural-language corpora. We address this gap with DualGraph, a RAG framework that represents documents through two complementary views: a Textual Knowledge Graph for semantic retrieval and a Symbolic Knowledge Graph for symbolic querying over typed subject--predicate--object triples. Building on these two components, we provide multiple strategies for selecting or combining semantic and symbolic evidence.We also introduce SpecsQA, a benchmark from a commercial shopping website with semi-structured product documents and manually curated questions spanning open-ended and specification-oriented retrieval. Experiments show that DualGraph consistently outperforms state-of-the-art dense-retrieval, GraphRAG, symbolic, and table-oriented baselines across question types.Code and data are available at https://github.com/corneliocristina/DualGraphRAG.
2605.27164.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）系統在回答問題時通常通過查詢與文檔片段之間的語義相似性來檢索證據。雖然這種方法對於非結構化文本有效，但在半結構化語料庫中，回答可能需要精確的過濾、聚合或對多個文檔的結構屬性進行徹底檢索時，這種方法的可靠性較低。符號方法支持這些操作，但在嘈雜的自然語言語料庫上往往不夠穩定。我們通過DualGraph來解決這一差距，這是一個RAG框架，通過兩個互補的視圖來表示文檔：一個用於語義檢索的文本知識圖譜和一個用於對類型主題--謂詞--對象三元組進行符號查詢的符號知識圖譜。在這兩個組件的基礎上，我們提供了多種選擇或結合語義和符號證據的策略。我們還介紹了SpecsQA，這是一個來自商業購物網站的基準，包含半結構化的產品文檔和手動策劃的問題，涵蓋了開放式和以規格為導向的檢索。實驗顯示，DualGraph在各類問題類型上始終優於最先進的密集檢索、GraphRAG、符號和面向表格的基準。代碼和數據可在 https://github.com/corneliocristina/DualGraphRAG 獲得。
2605.27164.author: Mateusz Czyżnikiewicz et.al.
2605.27164.authors: Mateusz Czyżnikiewicz, Ryszard Tuora, Adam Kozakiewicz, Tomasz Ziętkiewicz, Mateusz Galiński, Michał Godziszewski, Michał Karpowicz, Timothy Hospedales, Cristina Cornelio
